user_story.action: Initiate a repair request with address and key details
user_story.outcome: System creates a Service Call Case Lifecycle record for the homeowner

acceptance_criteria.description: Request creation is recorded and verified
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: POST /repair-requests returns 201 and record exists in database within '1 second'

acceptance_criteria.description: Homeowner receives confirmation for the new request
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Notification message delivered to user inbox within '15 minutes'

user_story.action: View status updates on active repair
user_story.outcome: User sees real-time progress and evidence updates on the Service Call Case Lifecycle

acceptance_criteria.description: Status updates occur automatically upon job stage change
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: UI reflects new status within '10 seconds' of backend state change

acceptance_criteria.description: Evidence is viewable before payment
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: GET /work-orders/{id} returns media list populated before invoice generation

user_story.action: View portfolio risk and budget variance metrics
user_story.outcome: Dashboard displays accurate risk scores and financial metrics for multiple assets

acceptance_criteria.description: Risk scores are accurate within 5%
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Calculated Risk Score deviates from Ground Truth by < '5%' across all assets

acceptance_criteria.description: Cost vs Budget variance is reported correctly
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Report variance matches GL records with '0.01' precision

user_story.action: Manage field schedule and dispatch
user_story.outcome: System assigns jobs and resolves schedule conflicts automatically

acceptance_criteria.description: Dispatch conflicts are resolved automatically
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System rejects booking attempt if technician is double-booked within '30 minutes'

acceptance_criteria.description: Schedule Slot is reserved and released correctly
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-SCHEDULE-SLOT availability updated within '2 seconds' of job assignment

user_story.action: Close job and upload compliance evidence
user_story.outcome: Evidence is immutable and compliance check gates job completion

acceptance_criteria.description: Evidence is immutable after upload
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System hash mismatch triggers rejection for modified files after initial storage

acceptance_criteria.description: Compliance check fails job if missing docs
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Job status remains 'Incomplete' if mandatory fields in ENT-MEDIA-ITEM are null

user_story.action: Review compliance documents and motions
user_story.outcome: System enforces verified document status for approval

acceptance_criteria.description: Only verified documents can be approved
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Approve action button is disabled for documents with 'Pending Verification' status

acceptance_criteria.description: Voting record is immutable
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-VOTE record hash is locked after submission and cannot be altered

user_story.action: Cast vote on annual budget
user_story.outcome: Budget passes only if 2/3 majority threshold is met

acceptance_criteria.description: Budget requires 2/3 majority vote
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System calculates weighted votes and blocks finalization if ratio < '66.67%'

acceptance_criteria.description: Signature linked to Trust Ledger
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-TRANSACTION generated for digital signature event with immutable timestamp

user_story.action: Report common issue or request repair
user_story.outcome: Request linked to tenant fees and logged in Comm Channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Tenant can view fees paid for request
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-TENANT display shows associated Assessment balance for active requests

acceptance_criteria.description: Communication is logged in Comm Channel
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-MESSAGE created in channel associated with ENT-ASSOCIATION ID

user_story.action: Run procurement cycle for supplies
user_story.outcome: PO stays within budget limits and vendor is pre-compliance verified

acceptance_criteria.description: PO stays within budget limits
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System rejects PO if sum exceeds remaining ENT-BUDGET allocation

acceptance_criteria.description: Vendor list is pre-compliance verified
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Vendors from unverified list return 'Vendor Not Authorized' status

user_story.action: Review audit logs and data
user_story.outcome: Discrepancies logged and access restricted by role

acceptance_criteria.description: All discrepancies are logged
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-AUDIT-LOG entry created for every data modification event

acceptance_criteria.description: Audit access is role-restricted
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Non-admin roles receive 'Access Denied' when querying ENT-AUDIT-LOG

user_story.action: Receive predictive maintenance alert
user_story.outcome: Alert sent 30 days before predicted failure to prevent cost

acceptance_criteria.description: Alerts are sent 30 days before predicted failure
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-AI-ALERT trigger time = Current Date - '30 days' relative to Failure Probability

acceptance_criteria.description: Preventive cost is lower than repair cost
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System recommendation logic indicates ROI > '1.0' for preventive action

user_story.action: Complete payment after job
user_story.outcome: Payment confirmed in real time and receipt generated for Trust Ledger

acceptance_criteria.description: Payment is confirmed in real time
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-TRANSACTION status updates to 'Success' within '500ms' of gateway response

acceptance_criteria.description: Receipt is generated for Trust Ledger
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Receipt document downloaded from server within '1 second' of transaction

user_story.action: Store metadata on digital asset upload
user_story.outcome: Digital asset rights flagged in metadata record for future decision

acceptance_criteria.description: Ownership rights flag persists in metadata
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-MEDIA-ITEM contains 'asset_rights_owner' field populated on upload

user_story.action: Connect to external data provider
user_story.outcome: Connection status tracked for integration readiness

acceptance_criteria.description: Provider API connection status tracked
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System status field reflects 'Active' or 'Inactive' for data source ENT-METRIC

user_story.action: Coordinate with external vendor for non-participant HOA
user_story.outcome: Workflow initiates for external entity without requiring direct HOA integration

acceptance_criteria.description: Workflow allows external HOA entity creation
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System creates 'External Entity' record type for HOA without direct account

user_story.action: Validate statutory deadlines for notices
user_story.outcome: System enforces hardcoded regulatory time limits on notices

acceptance_criteria.description: Notice deadlines hardcoded to statutory values
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System prevents sending notice if current date + 'Notice Period' < Statutory Deadline

user_story.action: Validate incoming address and details against canonical schema
user_story.outcome: Request proceeds only if all fields match CDM patterns

acceptance_criteria.description: Validation rejects malformed address strings.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: HTTP 400 returned for any field failing Zod schema validation

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Input validation is a concrete implementation decision (Tier C) that gates the persistence flow; without this, DB consistency is violated.

user_story.action: Persist new Repair Request record to database
user_story.outcome: Record exists in database within '1 second' of request

acceptance_criteria.description: Record is inserted into ENT-REPAIR-REQUEST table.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM repair_requests WHERE id = NEW_ID returns 1 within 1000ms of POST

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Persistence is the core state-change commitment required to satisfy AC-001; this is implementation-specific (Tier C).

user_story.action: Queue delivery of confirmation to user inbox
user_story.outcome: Notification message delivered to user inbox within '15 minutes'

acceptance_criteria.description: User receives email or push notification.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-NOTIFICATION entity created with status 'SENT' or 'DELIVERED' within 900000ms of event

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Notification is an atomic delivery task (Tier C) distinct from the core case creation; timing constraint is verified.

user_story.action: Trigger WF-1 (Intake Verification) durable workflow
user_story.outcome: Workflow instance starts immediately to begin verification

acceptance_criteria.description: Workflow DBOS instance initialized.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: DBOS workflow 'WF-1' shows status 'RUNNING' in metadata store within 50ms of persistence

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Workflow initiation is a specific architectural consequence (Tier C) of record creation, ensuring the verification flow begins.

user_story.action: Enforce 10-second latency budget on status event propagation from backend to UI
user_story.outcome: Every status change event triggers an update signal that reaches the client view within 10 seconds of backend state transition

acceptance_criteria.description: Status update latency is bounded at 10 seconds.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: timestamp(Client UI Render) - timestamp(Backend Event Persisted) <= 10000ms

nodes.decomposition_rationale: AC-003 specifies a hard latency constraint; this child commits to the architectural strategy (push vs poll) required to meet it.

user_story.action: Configure media list inclusion in work-order endpoint prior to Invoice generation
user_story.outcome: GET /work-orders/{id} includes populated media list regardless of payment state

acceptance_criteria.description: Media list is populated before invoice generation.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT media_list FROM work_orders WHERE id = '{id}' AND invoice_state IS NULL ORDER BY id LIMIT 1 HAVING media_list IS NOT NULL

nodes.decomposition_rationale: AC-004 defines data access policy (evidence visibility) independent of financial state; this child commits to the implementation of the query logic.

user_story.action: Implement SSE or Polling strategy for UI refresh based on event bus topic availability
user_story.outcome: UI reflects new status without excessive battery drain or polling overhead

acceptance_criteria.description: UI refresh mechanism supports event bus or polling configuration.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System supports event-streaming protocol (SSE/WSP) or configured polling interval < 5 seconds

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Selects the specific communication mechanism (Engineering Sub-strategy) to satisfy the latency constraint of Child C.001 without consuming mobile resources excessively.

user_story.action: Invoke Risk Prediction Engine (WF-8) to compute asset-level risk scores
user_story.outcome: Risk Score entity (ENT-RISK-SCORE) populated with calculated probability value

acceptance_criteria.description: Risk score matches WF-8 output within acceptable latency
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-RISK-SCORE.value === WF-8.predicted_value AND (abs(ENT-RISK-SCORE.value - ground_truth) / ground_truth) < 0.05

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Risk scores are the result of a specific calculation engine (WF-8); this child commits to using that specific source rather than a manual or alternative calculation.

user_story.action: Calculate Cost vs Budget variance using General Ledger records
user_story.outcome: Variance metric displayed against BUDGET plan

acceptance_criteria.description: Variance matches GL records with 0.01 precision
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: abs(variance_reported - (sum(actual_spends) - sum(plan_budget))) < 0.01

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Budget variance is a financial accounting commitment (Tier B) but decomposes into a specific implementation choice: summing GL transactions versus other estimation methods (Tier C).

user_story.action: Aggregate metrics into single ENT-METRIC view for Investor UI
user_story.outcome: Dashboard renders combined view of Risk and Variance

acceptance_criteria.description: Aggregated data reflects latest state without data loss
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: dashboard_metrics.id === ENT-METRIC.unique_id AND dashboard_metrics.timestamp > ENT-RISK-SCORE.last_update AND dashboard_metrics.last_gl_sync > ENT-ACCOUNT.last_posting

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This child binds the presentation layer (UI) to the data logic, enforcing the architectural choice that dashboard metrics are derived from downstream entities rather than being stored redundantly.

user_story.action: reject booking attempts if technician is double-booked within 30 minutes
user_story.outcome: Invalid booking requests are immediately denied by the Dispatch Engine

acceptance_criteria.description: System rejects booking if conflict exists.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: booking_request.rejected == true if exists overlapping SLOT within 30 minutes

nodes.decomposition_rationale: AC-007 defines the conflict resolution policy; this child commits to the specific rejection logic implementation.

user_story.action: update schedule slot availability immediately after job assignment
user_story.outcome: SLOT availability reflects new assignment without race conditions

acceptance_criteria.description: Availability updates within 2 seconds.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: timestamp(slot_updated) - timestamp(job_assigned) <= 2000ms

nodes.decomposition_rationale: AC-008 defines the latency requirement; this child commits to the implementation of the slot state propagation logic.

user_story.action: verify technician qualifications against job requirements
user_story.outcome: Only qualified and active technicians are considered for assignment

acceptance_criteria.description: Unqualified technicians are excluded from candidate pool.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: technician.eligible == true implies technician.license == job.license_requirement

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This child commits to the pre-assignment eligibility filtering strategy to ensure compliance and safety.

user_story.action: utilize WF-3 Dispatch Scheduling Engine for all scheduling logic
user_story.outcome: All dispatch decisions are routed through the durable workflow engine

acceptance_criteria.description: Dispatch logic executes within the WF-3 workflow version.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: workflow_execution_id == 'WF-3' for every dispatch event

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This is a commitment on the architectural infrastructure (DBOS/Workflow) used to implement the scheduling logic.

user_story.action: enforce write-once-read-many storage constraint on evidence files
user_story.outcome: Modified files trigger hash mismatch and storage rejection

acceptance_criteria.description: Upload request rejected if file hash on disk differs from hash at ingest time
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: sha256(file_at_storage) === hash_in_db_entry

nodes.decomposition_rationale: AC-009 requires a specific storage behavior (immutability). This child commits to the storage layer strategy to ensure that verification is not just a UI check but a storage constraint.

user_story.action: define mandatory documentation rules for job completion
user_story.outcome: Job status transitions to 'Incomplete' if required media attributes are missing

acceptance_criteria.description: Job status query returns 'Incomplete' when ENT-MEDIA-ITEM mandatory columns contain NULL
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT status FROM work_orders WHERE media_id IN (SELECT id FROM media_items WHERE required_field IS NULL) = 'Incomplete'

nodes.decomposition_rationale: AC-010 describes a business rule defining the system state policy. It determines what constitutes 'compliance' rather than how the system technically stores data.

user_story.action: compute and persist cryptographic hash on upload endpoint
user_story.outcome: Audit trail contains proof of file content at time of ingestion

acceptance_criteria.description: Hash string generated and stored before job transition to 'Closed'
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: work_orders.cryptographic_hash IS NOT NULL AND work_orders.upload_timestamp <= work_orders.hash_computed_timestamp

nodes.decomposition_rationale: To support the immutability commitment, the system must explicitly calculate and store the hash. This is a concrete implementation decision on the endpoint logic.

user_story.action: Disable approve control for pending documents
user_story.outcome: User cannot modify motion state to approved without verification

acceptance_criteria.description: Approve button disabled for unverified status
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: UI button state === 'disabled' if ENT-DOCUMENT.status !== 'Verified'

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Implements AC-011 directly; binds the UI behavior to the document state without defining the state logic itself, ensuring the policy is executable at the interface layer.

user_story.action: Lock voting record hash on write
user_story.outcome: Vote record hash remains constant across all reads

acceptance_criteria.description: Stored hash matches current vote data
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: hash(ENT-VOTE.data) === ENT-VOTE.store_hash for every read operation

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Implements AC-012 directly; enforces the immutability commitment via a concrete data integrity check that is testable independently of the workflow logic.

user_story.action: Ensure workflow status propagation before approval
user_story.outcome: Verification status update is visible before approval action is accepted

acceptance_criteria.description: Status query returns latest workflow state
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: read(ENT-DOCUMENT.status) returns current WF-5 state within 500ms of transaction commit

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Defines the sub-area of status consistency required to support the Tier C implementations; commits to the timing constraint needed for the UI logic to function correctly.

user_story.action: Calculate and validate weighted vote ratio on finalization
user_story.outcome: Budget finalization fails if weighted vote ratio is below 66.67%

acceptance_criteria.description: Vote ratio threshold is enforced
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: sum(ENT-VOTE.weight) / ENT-VOTE.total_weight >= 0.6667 for all votes cast

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This is the primary implementation commitment to enforce the policy choice in the Acceptance Criteria (AC-013). It commits to the specific algorithm for calculating the ratio.

user_story.action: Generate Trust Ledger transaction for vote signature
user_story.outcome: Every vote cast generates an immutable ENT-TRANSACTION record

acceptance_criteria.description: Signature event linked to budget finalization
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-TRANSACTION.id generated and timestamp < ENT-BUDGET.finalization_time

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This is the primary implementation commitment to enforce the policy choice in Acceptance Criteria (AC-014). It commits to the specific data model (ENT-TRANSACTION) and temporal relationship for compliance.

user_story.action: Persist individual vote record cast
user_story.outcome: Vote cast is recorded in ENT-VOTE entity

acceptance_criteria.description: Vote record is created for cast
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: INSERT INTO ENT-VOTE returns unique row with voter_id, vote_option, and hash

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This is an atomic action (Leaf) representing the storage consequence of a user cast vote. It is testable independently of the ratio calculation logic.

user_story.action: Enforce read-only budget state when threshold not met
user_story.outcome: ENT-BUDGET status remains non-finalized

acceptance_criteria.description: Budget status blocked from change
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-BUDGET.status = 'PENDING' OR 'VOTING' when vote_ratio < 0.6667

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This is the verification condition for the guardrail logic. It ensures the system state adheres to the 2/3 majority constraint defined in the parent.

user_story.action: Bind Repair Request to Tenant Assessment Entity
user_story.outcome: The repair request record references the specific assessment account for the requesting tenant

acceptance_criteria.description: Request record references a valid assessment account for the tenant.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: request_record.assessment_id === tenant_record.assessment_id AND assessment_status is ACTIVE

nodes.decomposition_rationale: AC-015 mandates fee visibility; binding the request to the assessment is the logical implementation that enables this view. This child commits to the data relationship required.

user_story.action: Persist Communication Event to Association Channel
user_story.outcome: A new message record is created and persisted within the active community chat thread

acceptance_criteria.description: Message record is created with the correct association channel context.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: message_record.channel_id === association_channel_id AND message_type === 'repair_report'

nodes.decomposition_rationale: AC-016 requires logging to a channel; this child commits to the persistence logic that maps the request to the association's specific communication infrastructure.

user_story.action: Render Fee Balance Display to Authorized Tenant
user_story.outcome: Tenant view renders the associated assessment balance for active requests in the UI

acceptance_criteria.description: Fee balance is displayed to the tenant who submitted the request.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: UI_display.balance_value === request_record.assessment.balance AND request_submitter_id === tenant_id

nodes.decomposition_rationale: The display operation is the atomic termination of the data flow; it is testable as a UI render action without further decomposition into UI component logic.

user_story.action: Validate ENT-BUDGET remaining allocation against PO total cost
user_story.outcome: Transaction commits only if cost <= remaining budget

acceptance_criteria.description: PO creation fails with budget error when sum exceeds limit
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: total_po_amount <= (ENT-BUDGET.total - ENT-BUDGET.spent) at write time

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Direct mapping of AC-017. This child binds the financial constraint to the transaction flow.

user_story.action: Verify ENT-VENDOR status in verification store
user_story.outcome: Returns 'Vendor Not Authorized' if entity not found in verified list

acceptance_criteria.description: PO creation blocked for unverified vendor
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: status(ENT-VENDOR) == 'AUTHORIZED' must be true before WF-11 invocation

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Direct mapping of AC-018. This child commits to the pre-compliance verification logic referenced in the user journey.

user_story.action: Initialize WF-11 Vendor Supply Chain Check for new PO
user_story.outcome: WF-11 status reflects verification progress for downstream steps

acceptance_criteria.description: Workflow instance created with valid context
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: WF-11 instance exists in DB with status CHECK_STARTED upon PO request

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Orchestrates the compliance engine (WF-11) required for the vendor check logic.

user_story.action: Persist ENT-PURCHASE-ORDER record to ENT-PURCHASE-ORDER
user_story.outcome: PO record exists with immutable timestamp

acceptance_criteria.description: Single PO record stored successfully
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT count(*) FROM ENT-PURCHASE-ORDER WHERE order_number = {PO_NUM} AND id IS NOT NULL = 1

nodes.decomposition_rationale: The terminal success path: the atomic data persistence event after all validation passes.

user_story.action: Ingest data modification events into ENT-AUDIT-LOG upon transaction commit
user_story.outcome: Every data modification event creates a corresponding entry in the audit log entity

acceptance_criteria.description: All discrepancies and modification events generate a log entry.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: count(ENT-AUDIT-LOG) >= count(data_modification_events) within any time window

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Distinguishes the logging mechanism (system action) from the access policy. This child commits to the ingestion strategy required for AC-019.

user_story.action: Enforce role-based access control (RBAC) on ENT-AUDIT-LOG queries
user_story.outcome: Non-admin roles are blocked from viewing audit logs, returning an Access Denied response

acceptance_criteria.description: Query access is restricted to authorized roles only.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: response.status === 403 if user_role NOT IN ['admin', 'platform_staff']

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Distinguishes the access enforcement logic. This child binds the system to the Cerbos engine (TECH-13) and policy logic required for AC-020.

user_story.action: compute trigger timestamp 30 days prior to predicted failure
user_story.outcome: Alert entry is queued exactly 30 days before failure_date

acceptance_criteria.description: Trigger timestamp equals predicted_failure_date minus 30 days.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: trigger_timestamp === failure_probability_date - 30

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This child binds the timing constraint from AC-021. It forces the system to implement a specific temporal window calculation before alert dispatch, relying on the AI engine output.

user_story.action: evaluate ROI threshold on preventive action
user_story.outcome: Alert only generated if cost(remedy) < cost(failure) and ROI > 1.0

acceptance_criteria.description: Alert generation logic validates ROI > 1.0.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: (repair_cost_estimate - preventive_cost_estimate) / preventive_cost_estimate > 1.0

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This child binds the economic viability constraint from AC-022. It rules out sending alerts for repairs with negative or marginal economic value, ensuring cost-effectiveness.

user_story.action: dispatch notification to Investor inbox
user_story.outcome: Alert delivered via ENT-NOTIFICATION channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Notification status reflects sent event in system log.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: notification_status === 'sent' AND notification_recipient_id === investor_user_id

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This child binds the delivery mechanism. It ensures the AI advisory output reaches the user via the configured notification channel associated with the User Journey.

user_story.action: Update ENT-TRANSACTION status to 'Success' on gateway response
user_story.outcome: ENT-TRANSACTION record reflects confirmed status within 500ms of network event

acceptance_criteria.description: ENT-TRANSACTION status reflects Success within gateway response time.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: transaction.status === 'Success' when queried within 500ms window of gateway_response_time

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Directly addresses AC-023. Decomposes the 'real-time confirmation' requirement into the specific state transition action.

user_story.action: Generate ENT-DOCUMENT (Receipt) for Trust Ledger
user_story.outcome: Receipt document is available for download immediately after generation

acceptance_criteria.description: Receipt document is downloadable within 1 second of transaction.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ent_document.is_downloadable === true within 1000ms of transaction_commit_time

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Addresses AC-024. Separates document creation from financial posting to allow distinct latency budgeting.

user_story.action: Validate Gateway Payment Integrity before Commit
user_story.outcome: Transaction is rejected or quarantined if gateway response indicates error

acceptance_criteria.description: No Success state is persisted for invalid gateway responses.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: gateway_response_code in ['error', 'timeout'] triggers reject path

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Ensures integrity of the 'Confirmed' state before writing to ledger or triggering receipt.

user_story.action: Persist Immutable Entry to Trust Ledger
user_story.outcome: Financial movement is recorded immutably with audit trail

acceptance_criteria.description: Transaction hash and timestamp are immutable upon commit.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: transaction_hash === SHA-256(source_data) and timestamp_generated_by_platform_clock

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Satisfies the 'Trust Ledger' requirement by committing to the immutability constraint defined in VOC-14.

user_story.action: Define ENT-MEDIA-ITEM schema extension for 'asset_rights_owner'
user_story.outcome: ENT-MEDIA-ITEM schema includes 'asset_rights_owner' column

acceptance_criteria.description: Schema validation confirms 'asset_rights_owner' column exists.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Query ENT-MEDIA-ITEM schema returns 'asset_rights_owner' field at all times.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This commits the specific data structure required by the parent, defining the 'what' before implementation details.

user_story.action: Configure ExifTool for metadata extraction on upload
user_story.outcome: Rights data populated from file headers automatically

acceptance_criteria.description: Upload completes with rights data extracted from file.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-MEDIA-ITEM.asset_rights_owner matches EXIF header value within 50ms of upload trigger.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Commits the technical approach for data ingestion, resolving the 'how' of rights extraction.

user_story.action: Enforce transactional persistence before upload commit
user_story.outcome: Rights flag persisted atomically with upload state

acceptance_criteria.description: No partial uploads occur where rights flag is missing.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Transaction commits only if ENT-MEDIA-ITEM.asset_rights_owner is non-null.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Commits the architectural choice for data integrity, ensuring the flag is part of the upload transaction.

user_story.action: Validate rights owner ID against ENT-USER or ENT-ASSOCIATION
user_story.outcome: Invalid owner references reject the upload

acceptance_criteria.description: Upload rejects if owner ID does not resolve to an entity.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: INSERT into ENT-MEDIA-ITEM fails if 'asset_rights_owner' value not found in ENT-USER or ENT-ASSOCIATION.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Commits a governing rule for data validity, ensuring rights are assigned to legitimate entities.

user_story.action: Sub-function: Manage Asset Rights Lifecycle
user_story.outcome: System supports future decisions on asset rights

acceptance_criteria.description: System allows querying assets by rights status.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Search API returns 'asset_rights_owner' value for all ENT-MEDIA-ITEMs.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Subdivides the parent's functional scope into the lifecycle management sub-area without committing to specific algorithms yet.

user_story.action: Implement secure authentication handshake
user_story.outcome: Provider API accepts requests with valid credentials

acceptance_criteria.description: API calls include valid Authorization tokens.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Request header contains 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' or 'Authorization: Basic <encoded>'.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Authentication is the prerequisite engineering sub-strategy; without it, the integration strategy cannot be enacted.

user_story.action: Poll provider endpoint for health status
user_story.outcome: System detects provider outages or latency spikes

acceptance_criteria.description: Health check succeeds within timeout window.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: HTTP 200 returned within 30 seconds of scheduled check initiation or webhook confirmation.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Monitoring is the implementation commitment that enables the 'Active/Inactive' state verification in ENT-METRIC.

user_story.action: Sync external data metadata
user_story.outcome: System metadata reflects provider catalog

acceptance_criteria.description: External entity list updates within SLA.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-METRIC record shows 'Inactive' status within 60 seconds if provider API returns 5xx error.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Data synchronization is the downstream consequence of a successful connection strategy; defines when the status changes.

user_story.action: Define External Entity schema fields
user_story.outcome: System creates record with specific limited fields

acceptance_criteria.description: External entity schema maps 1:1 with external vendor JSON payload
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: schema.fields === vendor.payload.fields for every created record

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Defines the concrete data contract for the 'External Entity' record type mentioned in AC-027; specific enough to test without further decomposition.

user_story.action: Define Vendor Coordination strategy
user_story.outcome: API handshake established for data sync

acceptance_criteria.description: Vendor API endpoint reachable and authenticated
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: HTTP 200 OK response received on POST to vendor integration endpoint

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Architectural choice on how coordination occurs (API vs Agent) without imposing specific tech stack yet, committing to external connectivity as the strategy.

user_story.action: Configure Workflow Trigger listener
user_story.outcome: Workflow initiates on external record creation event

acceptance_criteria.description: Workflow status changes to 'Initiated' upon event receipt
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Workflow state transition to 'Initiated' occurs within 50ms of event emission

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Implements the 'Workflow initiates' AC; commits to a specific trigger logic mechanism tied to the external record event.

user_story.action: Enforce Data Isolation policy
user_story.outcome: External data does not mix with internal accounts

acceptance_criteria.description: External entity records are not visible to internal user portals
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT * FROM external_entities WHERE user_role in ('owner','tenant') returns 0 rows

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Governing rule ensuring non-participant HOAs remain isolated from core governance flows per 'without requiring direct HOA integration' AC.

user_story.action: Map specific statutory deadlines to each notice type (e.g., Violation Notice vs. Budget Notice).
user_story.outcome: All notice types have a defined regulatory deadline associated with them for validation

acceptance_criteria.description: Every notice type is linked to a statutory rule in the policy mapping.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Query ENT-NOTIFICATION type definitions returns a non-empty 'regulatory_deadline' or 'statutory_code' reference.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Deciding which law applies to which notice is a policy choice (Tier B) rather than an implementation detail. This separates the rule definition from the enforcement logic.

user_story.action: Enforce deadline check at the moment of notice generation or queue insertion.
user_story.outcome: System prevents a notice from being queued if the calculated date exceeds the statutory deadline

acceptance_criteria.description: Notice transaction fails validation if deadline is not met.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System returns validation_error.code == 'DEADLINE_EXCEEDED' if (current_date - created_date) > statutory_limit.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This child commits to the technical strategy of 'validation at commit time' which is testable and distinct from the policy mapping.

user_story.action: Reject the specific notice submission request when deadline is exceeded.
user_story.outcome: Client receives an immediate error response preventing the save operation.

acceptance_criteria.description: API request returns 400 Bad Request for expired notice.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: HTTP status 400 and body contains message 'Notice Period Expiry Prevented'.

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This is the terminal leaf operation that represents the system's refusal state, meeting the leaf criteria where further decomposition yields no atomic actions.

user_story.action: check address string against canonical Zod regex patterns
user_story.outcome: Address string conforms to canonical address schema format

acceptance_criteria.description: Address Line 1/2/3 matches Zod regex.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: address.address_line[1-3].matches(canonical_address_schema) === true

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Splits the high-level schema validation commitment into specific field-level string validation checks, which are individually testable leaf operations.

user_story.action: verify property ID validity against ENT-PROPERTY registry
user_story.outcome: Provided property ID exists in ENT-PROPERTY database

acceptance_criteria.description: Property ID is resolved in ENT-PROPERTY table.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: property_id in (SELECT id FROM properties) === true

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Separates the property existence check from the string format check, acknowledging A-0001's requirement that validation relies on property lookup.

user_story.action: map Zod schema errors to HTTP 400 response code
user_story.outcome: Client receives HTTP 400 when schema validation fails

acceptance_criteria.description: Response status is 400 on validation failure.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: http_status_code === 400 AND response_body.errors !== null

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Defines the architectural decision (implementation commitment) on how to signal validation failure, committing to a specific HTTP status mapping strategy.

user_story.action: Define latency measurement boundary
user_story.outcome: System clock used for latency calculation is agreed upon

acceptance_criteria.description: Latency measured from origin server receipt of request.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: start_time === request_timestamp_at_origin_server

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Defines the architectural boundary for the 1-second SLA, ruling out client-side measurement which would introduce uncontrolled variance or require NTP trust assumptions.

user_story.action: Enforce schema validation on incoming payload
user_story.outcome: Invalid payloads are rejected before persistence

acceptance_criteria.description: All payload fields match CDM patterns.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: payload === validate_schema(payload)

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Ensures data quality and prevents dirty data from entering the persistence engine; a concrete implementation choice regarding validation logic.

user_story.action: Write row to ENT-REPAIR-REQUEST table
user_story.outcome: Record exists in database immediately after commit

acceptance_criteria.description: Row exists in table within 1000ms.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM repair_requests WHERE id = NEW_ID returns 1 at commit_time + 1000ms

nodes.decomposition_rationale: The atomic action of state change; the most fundamental verification that the persistence requirement was met.

user_story.action: Log persistence event to audit trail
user_story.outcome: Commit event is recorded for forensic analysis

acceptance_criteria.description: Log entry created at transaction commit.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-AUDIT-LOG entry created at same timestamp as transaction commit

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Ensures compliance with audit requirements by capturing the exact timing and success of the persistence operation.

user_story.action: Create ENT-NOTIFICATION record with initial status on event trigger
user_story.outcome: ENT-NOTIFICATION record exists with status 'QUEUED' or 'SENT' immediately after event

acceptance_criteria.description: Record persists to ENT-NOTIFICATION table within 100ms of event.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ENT-NOTIFICATION WHERE event_id = ? AND status IN ('QUEUED','SENT') GROUP BY status HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This atomic DB write initiates the tracking lifecycle; verification can be performed immediately without external dependencies.

user_story.action: Publish message to delivery queue topic
user_story.outcome: Message event is visible in the queue for processing within 50ms of creation

acceptance_criteria.description: Message successfully published to internal queue without error.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: queue_count(topic) > 0 after 50ms post-insert

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Decouples database commit from external delivery; aligns with [A-0002] scope constraint that delivery is complete upon insertion.

user_story.action: Invoke external SMTP or Push SDK to deliver payload
user_story.outcome: Payload sent to external provider or device token endpoint within 900000ms of event

acceptance_criteria.description: External provider SDK returns success or enters retry queue.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: sdk_response.status_code in (200, 202) OR retry_queue_count > 0 within 900s

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Performs the actual outbound delivery; measurable against SLA threshold of 15 minutes.

user_story.action: Update ENT-NOTIFICATION status to 'DELIVERED' on success
user_story.outcome: Status field reflects 'DELIVERED' or 'SENT' based on outcome

acceptance_criteria.description: ENT-NOTIFICATION status updated to final state.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: UPDATE ENT-NOTIFICATION SET status = 'DELIVERED' WHERE id = ?

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Finalizes the record state; allows verification that the SLA was met or failure recorded.

user_story.action: Validate Repair Request context and payload for eligibility
user_story.outcome: Trigger request validated against ENT-REPAIR-REQUEST schema constraints

acceptance_criteria.description: Trigger payload validated against Entitlement rules.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-REPAIR-REQUEST record exists and is associated with valid ENT-HOMEOWNER before spawn

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Ensures only valid request contexts are escalated to the workflow engine, preventing invalid state propagation.

user_story.action: Initiate DBOS Workflow Instance
user_story.outcome: DBOS 'RUNNING' instance registered in metadata store

acceptance_criteria.description: Workflow engine acknowledges spawn request.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Workflow instance ID returned by DBOS API matches expected pattern within 5ms

nodes.decomposition_rationale: The core action of the parent requirement is the execution of the durable workflow engine; this child captures the engine interaction step.

user_story.action: Update Metadata Store Status Flag
user_story.outcome: External read sees 'RUNNING' status on metadata table

acceptance_criteria.description: Status query reads 'RUNNING' immediately.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT status FROM workflow_registry WHERE instance_id=... returns 'RUNNING' within 45ms of spawn ACK

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Satisfies the latency AC by ensuring the 'shows status' criterion is met before the 50ms deadline.

user_story.action: Persist status event with precise UTC timestamp at database commit
user_story.outcome: The `event_persisted_time` field is set exactly at the moment of the DB transaction commit in the primary region

acceptance_criteria.description: Backend event timestamp matches origin server clock within 1ms precision.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: abs(DB COMMIT TIMESTAMP - SYSTEM CLOCK AT COMMIT) <= 1ms for every event log entry

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Establishes the 'start point' of the latency budget. Without an accurate server-side timestamp, the 'backend event persisted' component of the AC cannot be reliably measured or enforced.

user_story.action: Serialize status payloads with minimal JSON footprint
user_story.outcome: Event message size does not introduce serialization latency or bandwidth throttling that violates the propagation budget

acceptance_criteria.description: Event payload serialization overhead is under 50ms on standard load.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: (Serialization_TIME - Event_Persisted_TIME) <= 50ms for every event batch under 100KB

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Addresses the network transport component. While C.003 defines the protocol (SSE/Polling), this child addresses the specific payload efficiency required to meet the budget under that protocol.

user_story.action: Process status update signal as high-priority UI task
user_story.outcome: The UI thread prioritizes the render of status changes over low-priority background tasks

acceptance_criteria.description: Client-side event parsing and DOM update completes within 100ms of network receipt.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: (UI_RENDER_COMPLETE_TIMESTAMP - Network_Response_Received_TIMESTAMP) <= 100ms on mobile devices

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Addresses the client-side rendering component. Since A-0004 states client-side time is included in the budget, this child commits to prioritizing the client thread to keep this portion of the latency under the 10s total.

user_story.action: Calculate end-to-end latency per event and compare against 10s threshold
user_story.outcome: System flags and logs any event that exceeds the 10s propagation window

acceptance_criteria.description: Latency violations generate an alert in the platform monitoring system.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: alert_count > 0 if (Client_Receive_Time - Backend_Persist_Time) > 10000ms within a rolling 1-minute window

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Ensures the 'verification' aspect of the parent requirement is actionable. This child implements the observation mechanism required to determine if the system is 'Enforcing' the budget defined in the parent.

user_story.action: Persist media_id reference in ENT-WORK-ORDER table during state update.
user_story.outcome: Work order entity records a valid link to media items upon creation or update.

acceptance_criteria.description: Every persisted work order contains a populated media_list reference.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: SELECT media_id FROM work_orders WHERE id = ? IS NOT NULL

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Binds the functional need for data presence to the data model layer, ruling out scenarios where media exists only in temporary storage.

user_story.action: Configure GET endpoint query to join media table independently of invoice state.
user_story.outcome: API response includes media_list array even for work orders with invoice_state.

acceptance_criteria.description: API response contains 'media_list' populated for work orders regardless of invoice_state.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: GET /work-orders/{id} returns non-empty media_list array when invoice_state != NULL

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Defines the specific API behavior required to satisfy the 'regardless of payment state' requirement, leveraging the existing join requirement in A-0005.

user_story.action: Validate media presence as a precondition for Invoice Generation trigger.
user_story.outcome: Invoice generation workflow validates media count before creating financial document.

acceptance_criteria.description: Invoice generation does not fail if media list is populated prior to trigger.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Workflow execution logs 'media_check_passed' flag before creating ENT-INVOICE record

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Ensures the 'before invoice generation' AC is met by adding a validation step in the workflow, rather than relying solely on storage logic.

user_story.action: Implement Server-Sent Events (SSE) endpoint with automatic reconnection logic
user_story.outcome: Client receives real-time status updates via persistent HTTP connection

acceptance_criteria.description: SSE stream adheres to protocol specification with defined reconnection headers.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: HTTP 200 response with Content-Type text/event-stream and Retry header present on every 200 OK

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Establishes the primary push mechanism defined in the parent. This child binds the implementation to the event bus publisher workflow.

user_story.action: Enforce maximum polling interval threshold of 5000ms for legacy fallback or connection loss scenarios
user_story.outcome: Polling frequency does not exceed system battery safety constraints or defined limits

acceptance_criteria.description: Polling interval variable capped at 5 seconds regardless of backend availability.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: System clock timer for poll loop is set via config key `polling_interval_max_ms` bounded by 5000

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Decides the boundary for polling behavior to prevent excessive battery drain mentioned in parent goal. Traces to latency budget sibling to ensure consistency in update timing.

user_story.action: Transition client to polling mode upon detection of SSE stream closure or error
user_story.outcome: UI continues to reflect state without total disconnect, utilizing polling as failover

acceptance_criteria.description: Client initiates HTTP GET request when SSE stream terminates.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Next HTTP GET request sent to work-order endpoint within 2 seconds of receiving SSE error event

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Ensures reliability (Service Continuity). Defines the transition logic from push to pull.

user_story.action: Adapt polling frequency based on device battery state (Background Fetch mode)
user_story.outcome: Reduced power consumption on mobile devices for users on low battery

acceptance_criteria.description: Polling interval increases when device battery level is below 20%.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Device Battery API check returns <20% value => Polling interval set to 60s instead of 5s

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Addresses 'battery drain' requirement in parent description. Requires OS-specific API interaction.

user_story.action: Extract and validate feature data for WF-8 inference
user_story.outcome: Clean feature vectors are ready for model consumption

acceptance_criteria.description: All required input fields are populated and typed correctly.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: input_data.schema_valid === true AND input_data.null_count === 0 at request time

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Input quality is a prerequisite for accurate WF-8 output; ensures the 'matches WF-8 output' AC is testable by ensuring the input matches expected schema.

user_story.action: Invoke WF-8 workflow and compute risk probability
user_story.outcome: Raw predicted probability value generated

acceptance_criteria.description: WF-8 workflow executes successfully and returns a value.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: wf-8.status === 'completed' AND wf-8.predicted_value IS NOT NULL

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Direct mapping of the parent's core function 'Invoke Risk Prediction Engine' to a specific implementation step using the named workflow.

user_story.action: Measure and enforce inference response time
user_story.outcome: Risk score returned within availability budget

acceptance_criteria.description: Inference completes within the system availability SLA.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: execution_time_ms <= 2000 AND execution_time_ms measured against origin application server clock

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Addresses the 'acceptable latency' clause in AC-010, utilizing existing A-0010 constraint for availability budget measurement.

user_story.action: Commit calculated score to ENT-RISK-SCORE entity
user_story.outcome: Risk Score entity updated with latest calculation

acceptance_criteria.description: Entity ENT-RISK-SCORE reflects the calculated value immediately.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: ENT-RISK-SCORE.value === wf-8.predicted_value AFTER commit AND ENT-RISK-SCORE.updated_at >= current_timestamp

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Addresses the 'so that Risk Score entity... populated' clause. Ensures the output is stored correctly for UI consumption.

user_story.action: Validate score deviation against ground truth or historical baseline
user_story.outcome: Ensured score accuracy remains within error margin

acceptance_criteria.description: Calculated score deviation from ground truth is within 5%.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: abs(ENT-RISK-SCORE.value - ground_truth) / ground_truth < 0.05 when ground_truth is available

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Addresses the accuracy clause in AC-010. Note: ground_truth availability is an external assumption.

user_story.action: Apply debit-credit balancing logic to actuals and budget
user_story.outcome: Variance metric is mathematically accurate based on committed GL entries

acceptance_criteria.description: Variance calculation uses (Sum Actual - Sum Budget)
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: calc(entropy(actual_spends) - entropy(plan_budget)) === variance_value for all rows at commit time

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Defines the core mathematical strategy for variance, distinct from data sourcing or display.

user_story.action: Fetch budget and actual GL records from committed state
user_story.outcome: System retrieves only posted records for accurate comparison

acceptance_criteria.description: GL records used must have status 'Posted'.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: query(SELECT * FROM ENT-TRANSACTION WHERE status = 'Posted') for variance computation

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Separates the data retrieval commitment from the calculation logic; enforces state constraint.

user_story.action: Inject variance value into ENT-METRIC entity for UI display
user_story.outcome: Investor UI renders the final Variance metric with correct formatting

acceptance_criteria.description: Variance displayed in ENT-METRIC view
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: GET /dashboard/metrics/US-003-2 returns variance field populated with AC-001 value

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Maps the calculated metric to the reporting view defined in sibling US-003-3.

user_story.action: Designate ENT-METRIC as the canonical aggregated view for Investor UI
user_story.outcome: All combined Risk and Variance data is persisted and retrieved via ENT-METRIC

acceptance_criteria.description: ENT-METRIC is the single source for the Investor Dashboard view
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: queries for investor metrics return data from ENT-METRIC.id

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Establishes the entity scope for the aggregation feature, distinguishing it from the raw data entities.

user_story.action: Enforce temporal ordering between dashboard metric and source risk score
user_story.outcome: Dashboard rejects stale risk scores where metric timestamp <= source last_update

acceptance_criteria.description: Metric timestamp exceeds risk score update time
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: dashboard_metrics.timestamp > ENT-RISK-SCORE.last_update

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Implements the first half of AC-012, defining the specific algorithmic verification for data freshness regarding risk data.

user_story.action: Enforce temporal ordering between dashboard metric and general ledger sync
user_story.outcome: Dashboard rejects stale GL data where metric sync timestamp <= account last_posting

acceptance_criteria.description: Metric sync timestamp exceeds GL posting time
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: dashboard_metrics.last_gl_sync > ENT-ACCOUNT.last_posting

nodes.decomposition_rationale: Implements the second half of AC-012, defining the specific algorithmic verification for financial data consistency.

user_story.action: Query ENT-SCHEDULE-SLOT records for overlapping time blocks
user_story.outcome: Return list of conflicting slots for current booking attempt

acceptance_criteria.description: All overlapping slots for the technician are retrieved within the request timeout.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Conflict query response time < 200ms and result includes all slots where (start_time <= request_time <= end_time + 30m) AND technician_id matches

nodes.decomposition_rationale: The logic to determine *if* a conflict exists is the primary sub-strategy for this requirement; it requires direct database queries into the schedule entity.

user_story.action: Calculate time difference against the active conflict window (30 minutes)
user_story.outcome: Determine binary state of 'conflict exists' based on UTC timestamps

acceptance_criteria.description: Conflict is flagged only if the proposed slot intersects the existing active window by the configured threshold.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: absolute(request_time - slot_start_time) < 30 minutes AND slot_status == active implies conflict == true

nodes.decomposition_rationale: The policy choice of 30 minutes is a constraint (A-0009), but the implementation of the comparison logic is an implementation commitment requiring a dedicated check.

user_story.action: Set booking_request.rejected flag and terminate booking flow
user_story.outcome: Invalid request state persisted, preventing further processing of the booking

acceptance_criteria.description: Request cannot transition to 'Confirmed' or 'In Progress' once rejected.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: booking_request.rejected == true AND workflow_state != 'confirmed' AND booking_request.id == [request_id]

nodes.decomposition_rationale: This is the terminal action (Leaf operation) that enforces the requirement outcome; it changes the request state permanently upon conflict detection.

assumptions.text: Address validation logic relies on property lookup in ENT-PROPERTY rather than strict string format checks alone.
Notification delivery is considered complete upon successful queue insertion, not external email server receipt.
User inbox access requires active ENT-USER session or verified device token.
Status update propagation latency budget includes client-side rendering time and network round-trip.
Media items for work orders are decoupled from Work Order state in storage, requiring a join or cache for UI retrieval.
Payment state is independent of evidence availability; evidence is visible regardless of financial posting state.
WF-8 Risk Prediction Engine output is treated as the sole source of truth for ENT-RISK-SCORE values.
Budget variance calculation requires GL records to be fully posted (committed) before dashboard retrieval.
The 30-minute conflict window is measured against active slot status, not calendar time alone.
The 2-second availability update budget does not include client-side render latency.
Technician qualification checks are performed synchronously before candidate generation.
Evidence storage layer supports object locking or checksum verification on write to prevent modification after initial ingestion.
Mandatory field definitions for evidence are stored in the database schema of ENT-MEDIA-ITEM.
Compliance check logic executes synchronously during the upload transaction before the DB transaction commits.
Verification status state change propagation occurs asynchronously relative to UI render time.
Document verification status is derived from WF-6 Safety Compliance Audit completion rather than manual flagging.
Voting weights are assigned uniformly to all Board Members without differentiation by property or class.
Trust Ledger immutable timestamp is generated by the platform's internal clock, not external.
The 66.67% threshold is calculated on decimal precision rather than rounded integers.
Repair request submission by a tenant persona implies ownership of the associated Assessment ID within the tenant record.
Comm Channel ID is derived directly from the Association ID via a static configuration lookup.
Tenant view access for repair request fees requires authorization check based on request_submitter_id.
Budget calculation excludes pending transactions not yet committed.
Vendor verification status is evaluated at the moment of PO creation request, not cached from a prior session.
Audit log entry creation happens at the transaction commit boundary to ensure immutability.
Role authorization checks are performed server-side via Cerbos policy engine before data retrieval.
Platform Staff persona requires elevated role privileges ('admin' or 'platform_staff') to query ENT-AUDIT-LOG.
Preventive repair costs are derived from ENT-PRICEBOOK at the moment of alert generation.
Receipt generation pipeline is asynchronous and does not block transaction commit success.
Gateway response parsing is performed synchronously before the ENT-TRANSACTION status update.
Rights owner ID must resolve to an existing ENT-USER or ENT-ASSOCIATION record.
Metadata fields are read-only after transaction commit to maintain audit integrity.
Upload transaction timing budget allows 10s for ExifTool extraction and write.
External provider API availability is treated as an out-of-scope dependency not guaranteed by the system.
ENT-METRIC schema must support an 'status' field that accepts 'Active' or 'Inactive' string values.
Authentication tokens must be stored securely and refreshed asynchronously before expiration.
Connection status updates occur at the database commit boundary to ensure consistency with audit logs.
External entity creation does not trigger internal financial journal entry immediately.
External vendor authentication relies on mutual TLS or shared secret keys, not standard SSO.
Statutory deadline values are immutable and stored outside the main write path (hardcoded or read-only config) to prevent modification by user workflows.
System clock synchronization (NTP) is maintained within <1 second of UTC for deadline calculations to be legally defensible.
Jurisdiction lookup is derived from the Association ID (ENT-ASSOCIATION) linked to the notice, not from the notice creator's location.
